revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:imported Kofun]]Mendorinagayama Kofun[[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 3]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. 3]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]]
{{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. {{Kofun navbar}} [[category:kk]] [[category:kk]][[category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[category:kk]] [[category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]] [[category:qq]][[category:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[category:qq]]

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
