{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
Mendorinagayama Kofun

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|07|43.9|N|136|04|42.9|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[Fukui, Fukui|Fukui]] in the [[Hokuriku region]] of [[Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|Scallop Kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[sarcophagus]] made of local [[tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18339569]] Wikidata name: Mendorinagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18339569]] Wikidata name: Mendorinagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18339569]] Wikidata name: Mendorinagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18339569]] Wikidata name: Mendorinagayama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuruga, Fukui|Tsuruga, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Fukui (city)|Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] made of local [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]] were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from [[:en:Fukui Station (Fukui)|Fukui Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Hokuriku Main Line|Hokuriku Main Line]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
