size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of local {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}} were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from {{ill|Fukui Station (Fukui)|en|Fukui Station (Fukui)|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of local {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}} were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from {{ill|Fukui Station (Fukui)|en|Fukui Station (Fukui)|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}} coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} made of local {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}} were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from {{ill|Fukui Station (Fukui)|en|Fukui Station (Fukui)|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of local {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}} were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from {{ill|Fukui Station (Fukui)|en|Fukui Station (Fukui)|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station|1=Fukui Station (Fukui)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mendori Nagayama kofun]][[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Mendorinagayama Kofun]][[ja:免鳥長山古墳]][[zh:免鳥長山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Mendorinagayama Kofun | native_name = 免鳥長山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Mendori Nagayama Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Mendorinagayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.128861|N|136.078556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tsuruga, Fukui|en|Tsuruga, Fukui|WD=Q847581|lt=Tsuruga, Fukui|1=Tsuruga, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Mendorinagayama Kofun'''|免鳥長山古墳||}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Medori neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Fukui (city)|en|Fukui (city)|WD=Q201618|lt=Fukui|1=Fukui (city)}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/212200 |title= 免鳥長山古墳 |trans-title= Mendorinagayama kofun cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Mendorinagayama Kofun is one of a cluster of tumuli located on a hillside approximately 900 meters from the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} coast. The cluster consists of five tumuli, of which only the largest is a {{nihongo|{{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|lt=scallop-shaped tumulus|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|帆立貝形古墳|hotategai-gata kofun}} and is protected by the National Historic Site designation. The remaining tumuli in the site are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (empun (円墳)) ''kofun''. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The largest of the group is Mendori No.5, which dates to the mid-5th century AD. The top was originally faced with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones and had cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. It has two horizontal [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s, but was robbed in ancient times. It was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from 2001 to 2006, and found to have two rectangular protrusions on its posterior domed portion. With the tumulus, fragments of a boat-shaped stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} made of local {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} and a decorated lid with a design of triangular and circular patterns, and fragmentary {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads have been found. Similar large-scale tombs of powerful local chieftains have been found in the Echizen area, but as a rule only in inland regions, such as the [[Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]] and the [[Matsuoka Kofun Cluster]]. This tomb is one of the largest of its type in the Hokuriku region, and is unusual in that the larger ''kofun'' found in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}} were all located in the eastern end of the Fukui Plain along the basin of the [[:en:Kuzuryu River|Kuzuryu River]], and only this one is located so close to the coast. It is located about 50 minutes by car from {{ill|Fukui Station (Fukui)|en|Fukui Station (Fukui)|WD=Q867992|lt=Fukui Station|1=Fukui Station (Fukui)}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Hokuriku Main Line|en|Hokuriku Main Line|WD=Q1318747|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|1=Hokuriku Main Line}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==The site== * Mendori No.1: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 38 meters. * Mendori No.2: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 20 meters. Its opening has been open since ancient times, and per local folklore was a hideout for pirates. * Mendori No.3: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 35 meters. * Mendori No.4: This is a circular tumulus from the 5th century, with a diameter of 28 meters. * Mendori No.5: Also called the Mendorinagayama kofun: This is a scallop-shaped tumulus from the 5th century, with a length of 90.5 meters
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[http://fukuisan.jp/ja/list/p001017.html Cultural Properties of Fukui city] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/fukui-mendorinagayama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
Mendorinagayama Kofun
